name: bespunky-voice
description: Hear Claude's questions and answer by voice — for when you're away from the screen. SPEAK: get a listenable, audio-dedicated summary of Claude's question (no markup or structure read aloud, just the gist and the options), on demand with `/voice say` or automatically with `/voice auto on` (default off) — a PreToolUse hook speaks multiple-choice questions and plan-approvals, and a Stop hook speaks plain free-text questions. ANSWER: `/voice answer` records your spoken reply, transcribes it locally, and hands it back as your answer. HANDS-FREE: the `ask_by_voice` MCP tool plus the `voice-conversation` skill let Claude ask a question aloud and BLOCK for your spoken answer with no command to run — say "let's talk by voice" and Claude routes its questions through it, matching your spoken choice to an option. TTS is a swappable boundary (a free espeak-ng floor on Linux / `say` on macOS, auto-upgraded to a local Piper neural voice via scripts/install-piper.sh, built to allow a cloud voice); STT is local whisper.cpp, opt-in via scripts/install-whisper.sh (base.en → small.en); listen.sh boosts the mic itself to beat WSLg's low input gain. Needs a synthesis engine, a player (paplay/aplay/afplay), Node, a reachable audio sink (WSLg's PULSE_SERVER in the BeSpunky devcontainer) and — to answer — a microphone plus whisper.
